name: Paper Search MCP
what_it_does: An MCP server that enables AI assistants to search and retrieve academic papers from multiple sources including arXiv, PubMed, bioRxiv, medRxiv, Google Scholar, IACR, and Semantic Scholar through a unified interface.
use_cases: Searching arXiv and PubMed for papers on a research topic directly from Claude or other MCP clients
Performing multi-source literature reviews across biomedical and CS preprint servers in a single query
Retrieving paper metadata and abstracts for citation and research synthesis workflows
Using Semantic Scholar's enhanced API for citation graph and influence data
not_for: Full-text PDF download and parsing workflows (retrieval focus, not content extraction)
Paid/paywalled journal databases like IEEE Xplore or Springer without Sci-Hub fallback
Real-time or news-style content (academic preprints only)
best_when: A researcher or AI agent needs to find relevant academic papers across multiple preprint and index databases without switching between browser tabs or APIs.
avoid_when: Full-text retrieval and analysis of paywalled papers is needed, or the workflow requires structured citation management integration.

auth.notes: Optional Semantic Scholar API key via environment variable for enhanced rate limits. Other sources require no auth.

pricing.notes: MIT license. Underlying APIs (Semantic Scholar) may have rate limits on free tier.

agent_readiness.error_message_notes: Async httpx-based; error propagation to MCP client depends on implementation quality.
agent_readiness.idempotency_notes: Search queries are read-only and idempotent by nature.
known_agent_gotchas: Sci-Hub inclusion raises legal concerns in some jurisdictions
Google Scholar scraping may be rate-limited or blocked without API key
No pagination for large result sets documented
Planned sources (IEEE, ACM) not yet implemented

security_score_components.security_notes: Academic paper search MCP. Semantic Scholar/arXiv access. Public research data — low security risk. Rate limits per API key. No PII involved.
